results.input: I want to build a team dashboard that shows our project status
results.simulated_output:
Checking for existing ideation output... none found. Proceeding with cold discovery.

[AskUserQuestion — Step 1: Problem + Users]

Who is the primary audience for this dashboard?
  (a) Engineering team members tracking their own work (Recommended)
  (b) Team leads / managers needing a project-level view
  (c) External stakeholders (clients, executives)
  (d) All of the above

What's the core problem this dashboard solves?
  (a) We can't see at a glance whether the project is on track (Recommended)
  (b) Status is scattered across tools and nobody has a single view
  (c) Managers spend too long pulling status reports manually
  (d) Other — describe

What does 'project status' mean to you — what data should it show?
  (a) Sprint progress (stories done vs total) (Recommended)
  (b) Bug counts and severity
  (c) Deployment / CI health
  (d) Team velocity over time

explanations.discovery_completeness: Response asks about problem, users, and data content (what 'project status' means) — three distinct discovery dimensions covered in the first interaction.
explanations.assumption_surfacing: Agent explicitly surfaces the ambiguity in 'project status' and 'team' by turning both into open questions rather than assuming sprint/engineering context.
explanations.scope_adherence: Response stays entirely in discovery mode — no artifacts created, no solutions proposed, no forward planning language.
explanations.question_quality: Questions are open-ended, specific, and targeted to discovery dimensions; option-based format follows AskUserQuestion convention correctly.

notes: First run — baseline.json updated with these scores.
